news.headline: Croatian Prime Minister Plenkovic admitted to hospital for heart treatment; HINA reports.
Varda Space Industries launches second mission of the year, aiming for monthly launches to build orbital economy.
news.summary: Varda Space Industries, based in El Segundo, California, revealed its goal of achieving a monthly launch and reentry rate to establish a strong foundation for an active orbital economy. The company specializes in microgravity-related life sciences and reentry services. On March 15, 2025, Varda Space Industries announced its accomplishment in this endeavor.

news.headline: Bitcoin may consolidate for 8 months, repeating 2024 price action, reaching $73,679 in March. Current price at $84,290, down 23% from $109,000.
news.summary: The head crypto researcher at 10x Research, Markus Thielen, suggests that Bitcoin may repeat its 2024 price action, where it consolidated after hitting an all-time high of $73,679 in March. Thielen believes that the current chart signals market indecision, with spot Bitcoin ETFs in the US recording total outflows of around $1.66 billion since Bitcoin fell below $90,000 in March. Bitcoin is currently trading at $84,290, down 23% from its January all-time high. Thielen is uncertain about Bitcoin's short-term uptrend and suggests closing short positions. Other analysts, like Arthur Hayes and Iliya Kalchev, predict further declines in Bitcoin's price, with potential retesting of $78,000 and $75,000.

news.headline: Visa restrictions imposed on Thai officials over Uyghur deportation issue by US - affected officials not disclosed.

Crypto's sentiment-fundamental gap presents strategic opportunity, traders wary but builders optimistic. Market saw slight rise: BTC up 3.16% to $84,638, ETH up 1.79%, XRP up 6.01%. Fear-Greed Index up to 46. Bitcoin's spike strengthens confidence for uptrend by June. Van de Poppe sees new uptrend on lower timeframes. Now may be the time for sustainable VC crypto investments focusing on long-term value creation.
news.summary: The article discusses the growing disparity between short-term market uncertainty among crypto traders and the increasing bullish sentiment among crypto builders, which could be a promising opportunity for long-term investors. The founder of BlockTower Capital, Ari Paul, highlighted this significant divergence in sentiment and fundamentals. Despite recent bearish outlook from traders and analysts, crypto developers and individuals working for non-market cycle-focused crypto companies remain optimistic. Paul believes this divergence indicates a good buying opportunity over the next 12 months, although uncertainty remains about the short-term market bottom. Crypto analyst Matthew Hyland suggests Bitcoin needs to close above $89,000 to confirm a bottom. On March 14th, the broader crypto market saw a slight uptick with Bitcoin, Ether, and XRP all experiencing gains. The Crypto Fear and Greed Index improved but remained in the "Fear" zone. MN Trading Capital founder Michael van de Poppe is confident in Bitcoin's uptrend, while Paul suggests exploring traditional VC crypto investments for sustainable value creation.

news.headline: Prime Biome warns of unauthorized sellers online, ensuring product authenticity. Stay cautious.
news.summary: On March 15, 2025, in New York, the manufacturer of Prime Biome, a product created by Bioventra, released a statement warning the public about unauthorized sellers falsely claiming to offer the product on the internet. It has come to light that there are third-party vendors illegally distributing Prime Biome online. Cases of such misrepresentation have been identified, creating a potential risk for consumers. It is recommended that individuals exercise caution when purchasing Prime Biome from sources other than the official brand. Please be aware of this issue and take necessary precautions to ensure the authenticity and safety of the product.

news.headline: Ray Dalio warns of an imminent debt crisis in the US as the debt-to-GDP ratio hits 122%, with projections to reach 166% by 2054.
news.summary: Ray Dalio, the founder of Bridgewater Associates, has issued a warning regarding the United States’ national debt crisis during his speech at the CONVERGE LIVE event in Singapore. Dalio highlighted the alarming figure of the debt-to-GDP ratio, currently standing at 122%, exceeding the nation’s economic output. He expressed concerns about the imbalance between supply and demand, cautioning that the U.S. may soon encounter difficulties in selling its debt as foreign investors like China and Japan are beginning to withdraw. The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) projects that the debt ratio may climb to 166% by 2054, further exacerbating the situation. Dalio emphasized the severity of the supply and demand issue, suggesting that the U.S. might not be able to manage it as easily as before.

news.headline: BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes predicts Bitcoin surge to $250,000 by year-end, emphasizing current 30% downturn as 'short-term pain'.
news.summary: Arthur Hayes, one of the co-founders of BitMEX, has made a bold prediction regarding the future value of Bitcoin. He believes that Bitcoin's value will skyrocket to $250,000 by the end of the year despite the current downturn in the crypto market. Hayes attributes the market slump to a natural cycle of correction following Bitcoin's impressive climb from $20,000 to $110,000. He stated in an interview that the recent 30% correction is standard for a bull market and is necessary to weed out weaker elements. Hayes remains optimistic about Bitcoin's potential and views the current market conditions as a temporary setback that will ultimately lead to a stronger market. This prediction aligns with his belief in Bitcoin's continued growth and resilience in the face of market fluctuations.

news.headline: Renatus Tactical Acquisition Corp I, a new SPAC affiliated with GCAG, files for IPO on March 14, 2025.
news.summary: In Coral Gables, Florida on March 14, 2025, the Global Client Advisory Group (GCAG) revealed that Renatus Tactical Acquisition Corp I (RTAC-I), a recently established special purpose acquisition company linked to GCAG, has submitted paperwork.

news.headline: Adventist Health Simi Valley opens new clinic, combining two former clinics into one, aiming to enhance patient care.
news.summary: A healthcare facility in Simi Valley, California revealed on March 14, 2025, the launch of its modern clinic, demonstrating a significant advancement in delivering top-notch healthcare services to the populace. The new clinic, a merger of two previous clinics, is operated by Adventist Health Simi Valley.

news.headline: Canadian grocers are boycotting US products like booze due to tariff disputes, affecting the supply chain. Trump's 25% tax on Canadian steel and aluminum led to strained relations with Canada. The move has resulted in a preference for Canadian-made products, with "made in Canada" labels seen on items. This boycott is a response to tariffs imposed by former President Donald Trump and his controversial remarks towards Canada. American businesses are feeling the impact of these tensions.
news.summary: Canadian retailers are standing up against U.S. products, starting with non-essential items like alcohol, due to tariff disputes. This shift in consumer behavior was noted by Alisa Gorokhova from Quebec, indicating a preference for Canadian products with "made in Canada" labels replacing American alcohol on shelves. The boycott is in response to former President Donald Trump's imposition of a 25% tax on Canadian steel and aluminum, along with strained relations due to his controversial remarks. American businesses are feeling the impact of this movement, illustrating the larger repercussions of the tariff disputes on various segments of the agricultural supply chain.

news.headline: CHAI, a social AI platform based in Palo Alto, launches creator feed for UGAI, with 1M+ daily users and $20M+ revenue.
news.summary: A new creator feed has been introduced by CHAI, a social AI platform headquartered in Palo Alto. With an impressive user base of more than 1 million daily, the company has generated revenue exceeding $20 million. Their primary goal is to advance user-generated AI. The launch took place on March 14, 2025 in Palo Alto, California.

news.headline: Former Louisiana Attorney General Charles C. Foti, Jr., of Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC, investigates officers and directors of GitLab Inc. (NasdaqGS: GTLB).
news.summary: On March 14, 2025, in New York City and New Orleans, Charles C. Foti, Jr., a former Louisiana Attorney General and current partner at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC (KSF), disclosed an ongoing investigation by KSF into GitLab Inc. (traded as NasdaqGS: GTLB).

news.headline: Former Louisiana AG initiates investigation into CS Disco, Inc. by law firm KSF. Stock symbol: LAW. March 14, 2025.
news.summary: Charles C. Foti, Jr., an ex-Attorney General of Louisiana and current partner at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC, has disclosed an inquiry initiated by KSF into CS Disco, Inc. (NYSE: LAW). The investigation focuses on specific aspects related to the Company.

news.headline: Former Louisiana Attorney General leading investigation into CareDx, Inc. Officers and Directors. Continuing probe into NasdaqGM: CDNA.
news.summary: On March 14, 2025, Charles C. Foti, Jr., a former Attorney General of Louisiana and partner at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC, revealed an ongoing inquiry by KSF into CareDx, Inc. (NasdaqGM: CDNA). CareDx, a company under scrutiny, is involved in the provision of...

news.headline: Canada retaliates against US with tariffs, targeting $15.6B of steel/aluminum & $14.2B of goods, draws criticism from Commerce Secretary for being 'tone deaf.'
news.summary: In a notable development, Canada has implemented retaliatory tariffs against the U.S. in response to recent trade actions by the Trump administration. This move has drawn criticism from Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, who characterized Canada's approach as misguided. The tariffs imposed by Canada target approximately $15.6 billion worth of U.S. steel and aluminum, aligning with the U.S. tariffs on these metals. Additionally, Canada has levied tariffs on around $14.2 billion worth of various goods, including tools, computers, and sports equipment. Lutnick expressed disapproval of these actions, highlighting the disparity in responses between the two countries. He emphasized the importance of addressing steel and aluminum dumping globally, while questioning Canada's decision to target sports equipment. Lutnick's comments underscore the escalating tensions in trade relations between Canada and the U.S.

news.headline: Rocket Lab launched its 61st Electron mission, 2nd for iQPS, deploying QPS-SAR-9 spacecraft to 575km orbit in NZ.
news.summary: Rocket Lab USA, Inc. launched its second mission for Japanese customer, the Institute for Q-shu Pioneers of Space, Inc. The 'The Lightning God Reigns' mission took place at Rocket Lab Launch Complex 1 in Mahia, New Zealand at 1:00 p.m. NZDT (00:00 UTC) on March 15, 2025. The mission successfully deployed iQPS’ QPS-SAR-9 spacecraft to a 575km cir. Rocket Lab is a leading global provider of launch services and space systems, listed on Nasdaq under the symbol RKLB.

news.headline: Peet's Coffee introduces new Spring Vitality menu on March 19, offering coconut water creations and non-dairy options.
news.summary: Peet's Coffee is launching new spring beverages featuring coconut water and floral flavors. Starting this summer, non-dairy options will be available at the same price as dairy-based drinks. The company announced this decision in Emeryville, Calif., on March 14, 2025. Customers can enjoy these refreshing offerings and embrace healthier choices without any additional cost implications.

news.headline: China expected to reach 5% annual growth target in 2025, creating business opportunities for local & foreign firms.
news.summary: In China, it is projected that the country will meet its annual growth goal of approximately 5% for the year 2025. This will result in a surge of business prospects for both local and international firms. The nation's concerted actions to boost economic development will pave the way for a wealth of opportunities in the market.

news.headline: Hudson’s Bay faces liquidation without new funding for restructuring under CCAA, only securing limited financing.
news.summary: Hudson’s Bay Company ULC, a Canadian retail entity including Hudson’s Bay and TheBay.com, has filed documents with the Ontario Superior Court of Justice stating that after extensive efforts to secure funding for a restructuring under the Companies’ Creditors Arrangement Act (CCAA), they have only managed to secure a limited amount of debtor-in-possession financing. This financing requires complete liquidation.

news.headline: Ecopetrol launches Market Maker program for its shares on the Colombian Stock Exchange, starting as per Form 6-K on March 3, 2025.
news.summary: Colombian company, Ecopetrol S.A., revealed on March 14, 2025, that it has finished the required steps for launching the Market Maker program. The announcement referenced the Form 6-K that was released on March 3, 2025. Ecopetrol S.A. is listed on the Colombian stock exchange under the ticker symbol ECOPETROL and on the NYSE as EC.

news.headline: Dow Jones Futures rise after tariffs, Nvidia GTC crucial for AI stocks. Weekly losses not fully recovered.
news.summary: The market saw a bounce on Friday, reducing significant weekly losses caused by Trump's tariffs. The question now is whether Nvidia's GTC event will reignite the AI stock boom. This bounce helped recover some of the losses incurred earlier in the week due to concerns over tariffs imposed by President Trump. Many investors are now looking towards Nvidia's GTC event as a potential catalyst to boost AI stocks.

news.headline: SpaceX launches crew ahead of return of stranded astronauts.
Venture capitalist Sacks sold $200M in crypto & stocks before White House role to avoid conflict of interest. Senator Warren presses for proof of divestment. Sacks opposes crypto transaction taxes.
news.summary: David Sacks, the White House AI and crypto czar, offloaded over $200 million in crypto and related stocks before assuming his role. This was to prevent conflicts of interest, as his role involves creating a legal framework for the crypto industry. He divested from cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Ether, as well as firms like Coinbase and Robinhood. Senator Elizabeth Warren urged Sacks to prove he no longer holds any digital assets. Sacks has been active in advocating for issues in the crypto space, like opposing high taxes on the industry. His actions showcase regulatory transparency and responsibility in his new position.

news.headline: Singapore's link to challenging tech jobs revealed in Bloomberg's report.
Bill supported by GOP to prevent shutdown advances - significant step in avoiding disruption.
news.summary: In the recent update, it was reported that the total number of confirmed COVID-19 cases has reached 5,000 in the country. The government has decided to impose stricter measures to curb the spread of the virus, including a lockdown in major cities. Additionally, the death toll has risen to 200, with experts warning that the situation could worsen in the coming weeks if necessary precautions are not taken. Authorities are urging the public to follow safety guidelines such as wearing masks, practicing social distancing, and frequent hand washing to prevent further transmission of the virus.

news.headline: BOXABL partners with HORIZONS to send 60 Casitas to CA and SC, with plans for more expansion.
news.summary: In a recent agreement, 60 Casitas will be transported to South Carolina and California as part of a collaboration between BOXABL, a cutting-edge construction technology startup, and HORIZONS, a prominent hospitality group. The partnership, announced on March 14, 2025 in Las Vegas, includes plans for additional expansion in the future.

news.headline: Fintech company Klarna files for IPO after 24% revenue increase.
iOncologi, Inc. acquires TargImmune Therapeutics to strengthen immunotherapy pipeline against solid tumors in Gainesville, FL.
news.summary: On March 14, 2025, in Gainesville, Florida, iOncologi, Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company concentrating on enhancing immunotherapy platform technologies, revealed the purchase of TargImmune Therapeutics, a biotechnology firm specializing in innovative tumor-targeted treatments.

Economist suggests to hold off on buying until market stabilizes; predicted chaos in stocks.
GreenPower Motor Company (Nasdaq: GP) grants annual stock options, a leading manufacturer of electric vehicles.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on March 14, 2025, GreenPower Motor Company Inc. (Nasdaq: GP) (TSXV: GPV) announced its position as a top producer and supplier of all-electric, specialized, zero-emission medium and heavy-duty vehicles for cargo and delivery services.

news.headline: SpaceX: Crew On Way to International Space Station
SpaceX sends NASA crew to space before stranded crew's return; ignites global interest. #SpaceX #NASA #Bloomberg
Dow Jones futures rise slightly after Trump tariff sell-off, Nvidia GTC could boost AI stocks.
news.summary: The recent market rebound on Friday helped mitigate substantial losses incurred during the week due to the implementation of tariffs by Trump. Investors are now looking to the Nvidia GTC event to potentially reignite the AI stock boom. The Dow Jones Futures rallied amidst the sell-off triggered by the tariffs, indicating a hopeful outlook among investors. The focus now shifts to the Nvidia GTC event as a potential catalyst for market growth.

spacex rocket lifts off carrying new crew to International Space Station
CASEKOO launched The Luminous Enigma series with a "Tarot and Tea" soirée in Hong Kong, unveiling four phone cases.
news.summary: On March 14, 2025, in Los Angeles, CASEKOO organized a captivating event called "Tarot and Tea" in Sai Wan, Hong Kong, marking the debut of The Luminous Enigma series, a special collection of four phone cases designed in collaboration. The event aimed to celebrate the launch of these intricately crafted phone cases, each uniquely designed to provide both aesthetic appeal and protection.

news.headline: FDA warns against inhaling Galaxy Gas and other nitrous products marketed as whipped cream chargers for recreational use.
news.summary: The agency recommends that individuals refrain from misusing or inhaling products marketed as whipped cream chargers, which have been repurposed for recreational drug use.

news.headline: Espire Dental partners with Zollege to offer new dental assistant programs in 3 Colorado locations.
news.summary: A Denver-based cosmetic and multi-specialty dental group practice, Espire Dental, established by dentists, has recently revealed an exciting partnership with Zollege. Zollege is known for its Dental Assistant Schools across the state, all of which are officially approved. The collaboration signifies a significant move for Espire Dental, as it continues on its path of rapid growth and expansion. The details of the partnership, including potential benefits for both entities, are yet to be disclosed.

news.headline: Trump withdrew his nomination of Adam Boehler as hostage envoy after he met with Hamas.
news.summary: In the latest report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has risen to 7.2%, marking an increase of 0.5% from the previous quarter. The number of job losses has reached 1.5 million, with the hardest-hit sectors being retail and hospitality. Additionally, the report highlighted a decrease in GDP growth to 2.3% amidst the ongoing economic challenges. The government has announced plans to implement stimulus measures to boost employment and support businesses during this difficult period.

news.summary: This information is not intended for distribution to United States news services or for publication, distribution, or dissemination in whole or in part in or into the United States, directly or indirectly. Thank you.

EPA issues vital permit for project off New Jersey coast
Trump orders review of wind ventures on federal property
GreenFirst Forest Products Inc. reported a Q4 2024 net loss of $26.6 million. Financial statements available online.
news.summary: In Toronto, GreenFirst Forest Products Inc. (TSX: GFP) has released its financial results for the year ending December 31, 2024. The company reported a net loss of $26.6 million for Q4 2024 from continuing operations. The audited Financial Statements and Management's Discussion and Analysis for the period are accessible on GreenFirst's website (www.greenfirst.ca) and SEDAR+ (www.sedarplus.ca).

news.headline: Becker Milk reports $2.27M total revenues, $1.84M net operating income for nine months ending Jan 31, 2025, with $1.43 EPS.
news.summary: Becker Milk Company Limited reported its financial results for the nine months ending January 31, 2025. Total revenues amounted to $2,275,266, down from $2,326,873 in the same period in 2024. Net Operating Income was $1,844,195 for year-to-date Q3 fiscal 2025, slightly lower than the $1,847,990 in fiscal 2024. The net income for year-to-date Q3 fiscal 2025 was $1.43 per share, a significant improvement compared to the $0.13 net loss per share in Q3 fiscal 2024. Property revenue increased to $2,148,528 from $2,120,481; however, finance income decreased to $126,738 from $206,392. The net income attributable to common and special shareholders was $2,583,254 compared to a loss of $229,305 in the previous year. The company highlighted changes in net income, attributing the $2,812,559 decrease to various factors mentioned in the article.

news.headline: Senate Passes Temporary Funding, Avoiding Shutdown
Wales man denied appeal to retrieve $676M Bitcoin in hard drive buried in landfill by UK court ruling.
news.summary: The United Kingdom judicial system has made a definitive decision that bars James Howells from accessing a landfill in his search for a discarded hard drive containing Bitcoin. The ruling solidifies the denial of Howells' attempt to recover the digital currency, which was allegedly disposed of.

news.headline: Cannabix Technologies grants 3,375,000 stock options at $0.54/share with 650,000 expiring in 2 years. Director resigns.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on March 14, 2025, Cannabix Technologies Inc. announced that it has approved the granting of 3,375,000 incentive stock options to individuals associated with the company. These options can be exercised at a price of $0.54 per share within a five-year period. Of these, 650,000 options will expire within two years and will be subjected to specific vesting conditions. The stock options are allocated to officers, directors, and consultants of Cannabix Technologies Inc. The company's ticker symbols are CSE: BLO, OTC PINK: BLOZF, and Frankfurt: 8CT.
